variant_label: Labor & Employment — Iraq foreign workers, baseline total before the remittance-curb exodus
notes: Baseline: Iraq had about 1.2 million foreign workers before curbs on remittances caused roughly 200,000 (mostly Egyptian) to leave. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.8]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Free oil provided by Iran to Syria, continued at 1986 level
notes: Free oil provided to Syria will continue at 1986 levels of about 35,000 b/d, worth about $190 million annually at an average price of $15 per barrel. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Value of Iran's free-oil subsidy to Syria, annualized
notes: The ~35,000 b/d of free oil to Syria is worth about $190 million annually, based on an average price of $15 per barrel. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Share of Iran export revenues turned over to the central bank
notes: About four-fifths of Iran's export revenues are turned over to the central bank, according to the regime's own data. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.10]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iran readily accessible official cash reserves, start of 1987
notes: Iran began 1987 with $2-2.5 billion in readily accessible official cash reserves. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.11]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iran gold, silver, and jewel reserves, start of 1987
notes: In addition to cash reserves, Iran held $2.3-2.5 billion in gold, silver, and jewels at the start of 1987. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.11]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iran claimed less-liquid assets (escrow accounts, uncollectable LDC loans)
notes: Tehran claims about $6 billion in less liquid assets, including funds frozen in escrow accounts and uncollectable loans to less developed nations. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.11]

variant_label: Military expenditure — Iran annual foreign-exchange spending on military equipment/supplies
notes: Iran spends about $4 billion annually in foreign exchange for military equipment and supplies. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.11]

variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Iran real per-capita GNP decline, 1987 estimate
notes: On the basis of the middle estimate of oil production and prices, Iran's real per capita GNP is estimated to decline about 8 percent in 1987, following an estimated 16-percent decline in 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.12]

variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Iran real per-capita GNP decline, 1986 estimate
notes: Iran's real per capita GNP is estimated to have declined about 16 percent in 1986, the prior-year baseline for the 1987 estimate. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.12]

variant_label: Prices — Iran black-market inflation estimate, 1987
notes: Inflation on the black market is estimated at 20 to 25 percent in 1987, following a jump of at least 40 percent in 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.12]

variant_label: Prices — Iran black-market inflation, 1986 jump
notes: Black-market inflation jumped at least 40 percent in 1986, the prior-year baseline for the 1987 estimate. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.12]

variant_label: Prices — Iran official price increase, 1987 estimate
notes: Strong economic pressures will probably force the government to allow official prices to rise by about 15 percent in 1987, following increases of only about 10 percent the prior year. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.14]

variant_label: Prices — Iran official price increase, prior year (1986)
notes: Official prices rose only about 10 percent in 1986, the baseline against which the 1987 ~15 percent increase is measured. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.14]

variant_label: Demographics & Population — Tehran-area families in single-room housing, ghetto areas
notes: The Iranian press reports almost two-thirds of the families in Tehran's sprawling outskirt ghettos live in single, small rooms. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.14]

variant_label: Infrastructure & Technology — Tehran officially estimated annual fires
notes: The poor quality of housing helps explain Tehran's officially estimated 6,000 fires annually. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.14]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Arab financial aid to Iraq since war start, cumulative total
notes: Since the start of the Iran-Iraq war, total Arab financial aid to Iraq has totaled about $33 billion. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Saudi Arabia + Kuwait share of cumulative Arab aid to Iraq
notes: About $27 billion of the ~$33 billion cumulative Arab aid to Iraq has been given by Saudi Arabia and Kuwait. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Financial aid to Iraq, 1986 total
notes: Financial aid to Baghdad probably totaled about $2.3 billion in 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Cash payments to Baghdad, 1986
notes: Cash payments to Baghdad in 1986 were about $1 billion, almost entirely from Saudi Arabia. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Military expenditure — Saudi/Kuwaiti-financed arms-purchase payments for Iraq via Egypt, 1986
notes: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait have also paid Egypt for some arms sales to Baghdad; this aid probably totaled at least $200 million in 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Saudi/Kuwait financial aid to Iraq, 1987 estimate
notes: Financial aid from Saudi Arabia and Kuwait in 1987 is estimated to total $2-2.5 billion, depending largely on the level of fighting. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.16]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Value of Iraqi gold reserves sold since mid-1986
notes: To improve its cash position, Baghdad has sold several hundred million dollars' worth of its gold reserves since the middle of last year (1986). [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.17]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iraq liquid foreign exchange reserves, January 1987
notes: Gold sales probably increased Iraq's liquid foreign exchange reserves to about $1.3 billion as of January 1987. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.17]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iraq total foreign assets, remainder mostly uncollectible LDC loans
notes: The balance of Baghdad's foreign assets -- about $1-1.5 billion -- is mostly uncollectible loans to Third World nations. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.17]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Arab financial aid -- Iraq
notes: Table 3 'Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87', column '1984' (1987 scenarios assume average oil exports of 1.75 million b/d), row 'Arab financial aid'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 3 "Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.18]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Arab financial aid -- Iraq
notes: Table 3 'Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87', column '1985' (1987 scenarios assume average oil exports of 1.75 million b/d), row 'Arab financial aid'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 3 "Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.18]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Arab financial aid -- Iraq
notes: Table 3 'Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87', column '1986 (estimated)' (1987 scenarios assume average oil exports of 1.75 million b/d), row 'Arab financial aid'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 3 "Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.18]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Arab financial aid -- Iraq
notes: Table 3 'Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87', column '1987 scenario ($18/bbl)' (1987 scenarios assume average oil exports of 1.75 million b/d), row 'Arab financial aid'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 3 "Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.18]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Arab financial aid -- Iraq
notes: Table 3 'Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87', column '1987 scenario ($15/bbl)' (1987 scenarios assume average oil exports of 1.75 million b/d), row 'Arab financial aid'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 3 "Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.18]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Arab financial aid -- Iraq
notes: Table 3 'Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87', column '1987 scenario ($10/bbl)' (1987 scenarios assume average oil exports of 1.75 million b/d), row 'Arab financial aid'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 3 "Iraq: Current Account Balance, 1984-87" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.18]

variant_label: Foreign aid — UK credit rolled over into 1987 for Iraq (pharmaceuticals/medical/capital equipment)
notes: The United Kingdom agreed in November 1986 to roll over into 1987 about $300 million in unused credits to finance the purchase of British pharmaceuticals, medical supplies, and capital equipment by Iraq. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.18]

variant_label: Foreign aid — US Commodity Credit Corporation loan guarantee ceiling for Iraq agricultural exports, 1987
notes: The US Commodity Credit Corporation will guarantee payment on up to $700 million in loans to finance agricultural exports to Iraq -- up from about $600 million last year. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.18]

variant_label: Foreign aid — US CCC loan guarantee ceiling for Iraq agricultural exports, prior year
notes: The prior year's (1986) US CCC guarantee ceiling for Iraq agricultural-export loans was about $600 million, versus up to $700 million for 1987. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.18]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Foreign exchange requirements, total -- Iraq
notes: Table 4 'Iraq: Foreign Payments, 1987', row 'Foreign exchange requirements, total'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 4 "Iraq: Foreign Payments, 1987" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.19-20]

variant_label: Military expenditure — Foreign exchange requirements -- military imports -- Iraq
notes: Table 4 'Iraq: Foreign Payments, 1987', row 'Foreign exchange requirements -- military imports'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 4 "Iraq: Foreign Payments, 1987" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.19-20]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Foreign exchange revenues, total -- Iraq
notes: Table 4 'Iraq: Foreign Payments, 1987', row 'Foreign exchange revenues, total'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 4 "Iraq: Foreign Payments, 1987" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.19-20]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Foreign exchange revenues -- Arab financial aid -- Iraq
notes: Table 4 'Iraq: Foreign Payments, 1987', row 'Foreign exchange revenues -- Arab financial aid'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 4 "Iraq: Foreign Payments, 1987" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.19-20]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Financial gap -- Iraq
notes: Table 4 'Iraq: Foreign Payments, 1987', row 'Financial gap'. [cite: CIA NESA 87-10025, Table 4 "Iraq: Foreign Payments, 1987" (billion US$), May 1987, PDF p.19-20]

variant_label: Military expenditure — Iraq foreign-exchange cost of the war, monthly average 1986
notes: The foreign exchange cost of the war in 1986 averaged about $375 million per month, or $4.5 billion for the year. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.19]

variant_label: Military expenditure — Iraq foreign-exchange cost of the war, annual total 1986
notes: The foreign exchange cost of the Iran-Iraq war to Iraq in 1986 totaled about $4.5 billion for the year (about $375 million/month). [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.19]

variant_label: Prices — Iraq inflation estimate, 1987
notes: Shortages probably will drive Iraqi inflation to 20 to 25 percent during 1987, from about 15 percent in 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.20]

variant_label: Prices — Iraq inflation, 1986
notes: Iraqi inflation was about 15 percent in 1986, the baseline for the 1987 estimate of 20-25 percent. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA 87-10025, "Iran-Iraq: Gloomy Economic Prospects" (information as of 1 April 1987), May 1987, PDF p.20]
